title: RAG Accuracy Overhaul
problem: Existing RAG API had ~50% answer correctness across global Oracle support queries.
approach: Redesigned chunking mechanisms, integrated new data sources, and tuned retrieval to better align context with query intent.
result: Answer correctness improved from 50% to ~80% — a 30-point gain impacting enterprise support quality globally.

title: Knowledge Management AI App
problem: Support teams lacked a scalable way to convert interaction history into reusable, structured knowledge.
approach: Built from scratch — structured output pipeline (Pydantic models) generating KB articles with title, cause, symptoms, and solution fields. Added summarization for agent handoffs.
result: Production application powering a customer-facing portal, directly accelerating support workflows and reducing resolution time.

title: RAG Evaluation Framework
problem: No systematic, repeatable way to measure RAG quality across the product surface.
approach: Sole engineer. Built a golden-dataset testing framework producing relevancy, correctness, completeness, context faithfulness, and hallucination rate metrics across multiple RAG APIs and OCI-hosted LLMs.
result: Repeatable, data-driven quality baseline enabling ongoing RAG improvement with measurable benchmarks.

title: Custom Open Web UI Deployment
problem: Multiple Oracle business units needed a unified, configurable AI chat interface integrated with internal RAG services.
approach: Forked and deeply customized Open Web UI with Oracle DB integration, custom pipe class implementing SSE streaming, query rewriting, and per-product routing with tailored system prompts.
result: Internal AI platform serving multiple Oracle business units and products, enabling self-serve AI experimentation at scale.
